{{Short description|Italian film festival in 2012}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Film festival |name = 69th Venice International Film Festival |image = VeniceFilmFestival69poster.jpg |caption = Festival poster |opening = ''[[The Reluctant Fundamentalist (film)|The Reluctant Fundamentalist]]'' |closing = ''[[The Man Who Laughs (2012 film)|The Man Who Laughs]]'' |location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] |founded = 1932 |awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Pietà (film)|Pietà]]'' |artistic_director= [[Alberto Barbera]] |host = [[Kasia Smutniak]] |number = |date = 29 August – 8 September 2012 |website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[68th Venice International Film Festival|68th]] | followed_by = [[70th Venice International Film Festival|70th]] }} The '''69th annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''', was held from 29 August to 8 September 2012, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]]. {{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/lineup |title=69th Venice International Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121014085605/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/lineup |archive-date=14 October 2012 |access-date=26 July 2012 }} American filmmaker [[Michael Mann]] was the jury president for the main competition. Polish actress [[Kasia Smutniak]] was the Host of the opening and closing ceremonies. The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[Pietà (film)|Pietà]]'' by [[Kim Ki-duk]].{{cite news |date=8 September 2012 |title=South Korean film 'Pieta' wins Venice top prize |url=http://seattletimes.com/html/entertainment/2019100523_apeuitalyvenicefilmfestival.html |access-date=8 September 2012 |newspaper=The Seattle Times |location=Venice |agency=AP}} The festival opened with ''[[The Reluctant Fundamentalist (film)|The Reluctant Fundamentalist]]'' by [[Mira Nair]],{{Cite web|last=Scarpa|first=Vittoria|date=23 July 2012|url=http://www.cineuropa.org/2011/nw.aspx?t=newsdetail&l=en&did=223714|title=The Reluctant Fundamentalist by Mira Nair opens the 69ths Venice Film Festival|work=cineuropa.org|access-date=24 July 2012}}{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-19421160 |title=''The Reluctant Fundamentalist'' opens Venice Film Festival | work=BBC News | date=30 August 2012}} and closed with ''[[The Man Who Laughs (2012 film)|The Man Who Laughs]]'' by [[Jean-Pierre Ameris]].{{cite web|url=http://www.sheknows.com/entertainment/articles/967243/venice-film-festival-unveils-2012-lineup |title=Venice Film Festival unveils 2012 lineup |work=sheknows.com |access-date=4 May 2018}} ==Juries== === Main competition (''Venezia 69'') === * [[Michael Mann]], American filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/juries/ |title=International Juries of the 69th Venice Film Festival |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121015125250/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/juries/ |archive-date=15 October 2012 |access-date=20 April 2018 }}{{Cite web|author=Staff writer|date=13 July 2012|url=http://www.cineuropa.org/2011/nw.aspx?t=newsdetail&l=en&did=223452|title=Venice 2012: President Mann's eight jurors|work=cineuropa.org|publisher=Cineuropa|access-date=14 July 2012}} * [[Marina Abramović]], Serbian performance artist * [[Laetitia Casta]], French actress * [[Peter Ho-sun Chan]], Hong Kong filmmaker * [[Ari Folman]], Israeli filmmaker * [[Matteo Garrone]], Italian filmmaker * [[Ursula Meier]], French-Swiss filmmaker * [[Samantha Morton]], English actress and director * [[Pablo Trapero]], Argentine director === ''[[Orizzonti]]'' === * [[Pierfrancesco Favino]], Italian actor - Jury President * Sandra den Hamer, Dutch director of the [[International Film Festival Rotterdam]] * [[Runa Islam]], Bangladeshi-born British visual artist and filmmaker * [[Jason Kliot]], American independent producer * [[Nadine Labaki]], Lebanese actress and filmmaker * [[Milcho Manchevski]], Macedonian filmmaker * [[Amir Naderi]], Iranian film filmmaker and photographer === Opera Prima (Venice Award for a Debut Film) === * [[Shekhar Kapur]], Indian film director, actor and producer - Smithers Foundation Award === * ''Low Tide'' by Roberto Minervini === Interfilm Award for Promoting Interreligious Dialogue === * ''Wadjda'' by Haifaa Al Mansour === Giovani Giurati del Vittorio Veneto Film Festival Award === * ''The Company You Keep'' by Robert Redford ** '''Special mention''': [[Toni Servillo]] for ''[[It Was the Son]]'' === Green Drop Award === * ''The Fifth Season'' by Peter Brosens and Jessica Woodworth ==Controversy over Golden Lion== The jury led by filmmaker [[Michael Mann (director)|Michael Mann]] originally was to award the top [[Golden Lion]] prize to [[Paul Thomas Anderson]]'s ''[[The Master (2012 film)|The Master]]'', along with the Silver Lion Best Director award and the Best Actor award.{{cite news|url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/venice-controversy-jury-yanks-top-the-master-368892|title=Venice Film Festival Jury Yanks Top Prize from 'The Master' (Exclusive)|work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]|date=8 September 2012|first=Matthew|last=Belloni}}{{cite news|url=http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2012/09/08/the-master-venice-film-festival-award_n_1867317.html|title='The Master' Venice Film Festival Controversy: P.T. Anderson Film Loses Award On Technicality|work=[[The Huffington Post]]|date=8 September 2012|first=Christopher|last=Rosen}}{{cite news|url=http://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/goldstandard/la-et-mn-venice-film-awards-20120908,0,36455.story|title='The Master' wins big at Venice ... and it could have been bigger|newspaper=[[Los Angeles Times]]|date=8 September 2012|first=Glenn|last=Whipp}} However, because of festival's new rule prohibiting pairing the Golden Lion with any other prizes, the jury was asked "to re-deliberate to remove" one of the awards for ''The Master'', and the Golden Lion was awarded to [[Kim Ki-duk]]'s ''[[Pietà (film)|Pietà]]'' instead. On the other hand, the jury was planned to award the [[Volpi Cup|Coppa Volpi for the Best Actress]] prize to [[Jo Min-su]], who acted Jang Mi-sun in ''[[Pietà (film)|Pietà]]''. However, because of the festival's new rule, the jury couldn't award a prize to Jo Min-su.{{cite news|url=http://entertain.naver.com/ranking/read?oid=047&aid=0002008247|title=김기덕 감독 황금사자상 수상 "한국 영화계가 해준 게 없다"|publisher=OhmyNews|date=9 September 2012|first=Hahoon|last=Sung|language=ko}} In the official reception which was held after the festival was over, many juries, such as [[Samantha Morton]] and [[Peter Chan]] met Jo, saying that "Jo's acting was touching that changed my life. I shed tears of Jo's play."{{cite news|url=http://entertain.naver.com/read?oid=001&aid=0005805247|title="조민수 여우주연상 만장일치..규정상 못줘"(Volpi Cup for Jo was unanimous...couldn't give a prize because of festival's rule)|publisher=[[Yonhap News Agency ]]|date=9 September 2012|first=Mina|last=Lim|language=ko}}{{cite news|url=http://entertain.naver.com/read?oid=404&aid=0000013005|title='피에타' 조민수, 베니스 여우주연상 불발 이유 '알고 보니…'|publisher=News 24|date=9 September 2012|first=Hyunmin|last=Park|language=ko}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category}} *{{official website|http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/}} *[https://www.imdb.com/event/ev0000681/2012/1/ Venice Film Festival 2012 Awards] on IMDb {{Venice Film Festival}} [[Category:Venice Film Festival]] [[Category:2012 in Italian cinema|Venice Film Festival]] [[Category:2012 film festivals|Venice Film Festival]] [[Category:2012 festivals in Europe|Venice Film Festival]] [[Category:2010s in Venice|Film]] [[Category:August 2012 in Italy]] [[Category:September 2012 in Italy]]
